task_updates.executor_notes: No further code changes were needed in this pass. The rerun suite kept the registry batching, schema storage, and stable subscribe/getSnapshot behavior green, so the prior DynamicEffectRegistry implementation still holds under the new integration coverage.

Moved the singleton swap onto the hook render path by calling `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` immediately after memoizing the registry. The reproduction script and the new integration test both observed `same:missing` on first render and `same:found` after async registration, which confirms the module singleton is installed before child render and that `useSyncExternalStore` still drives the rerender path.
No new edits were required. The expanded DynamicEffectRegistry test module still exercises the boundary fallback path, so the runtime-throw degradation remains verified after the registry rework.
No new edits were required. The coercion assertions remained green in the updated DynamicEffectRegistry test module, so runtime param validation still behaves as implemented earlier.
No new edits were required. Full-suite verification passed again without changing the `wrapWithClipEffects` caller contract, so the error-boundary and param-validation wiring remains intact.

No new edits were required. The full video-editor suite stayed green, which revalidates that `EffectLayerSequence` still preserves its `effectFrames={durationInFrames}` contract while using the boundary fallback path.
No new edits were required. The broader rerun did not surface regressions in the preview composition path, so the existing wrapPreviewEffect changes remain intact.
No new edits were required. The rerun kept the effect-system modules clean, and the legacy hook deprecation remains unchanged.

Added the missing rendered-tree integration coverage in the existing DynamicEffectRegistry test file. The new test renders through the real `useEffectRegistry` hook, proves the module singleton matches the provider registry before child render, and then waits for async resource registration to update the DOM, closing the reviewer gap without adding a new test file.

Reran the full `src/tools/video-editor/` suite after the rework and it passed cleanly: 22 files, 71 tests. `npx tsc --noEmit` also passed, and no signature regressions were introduced at the `wrapWithClipEffects` call sites.

Wrote and ran a short reproduction script for the two reviewer-reported failures: first-render registry installation and rendered-tree reactivity after async registration. The script passed, was deleted afterward, and the subsequent targeted and full verification reruns found no additional regressions to fix.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Reverified that `DynamicEffectRegistry` still exposes stable arrow-field `subscribe`/`getSnapshot` methods and that batching collapses intermediate notifications into one flush.

This pass intentionally superseded the earlier `useLayoutEffect` guidance: `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` now runs during render, `useSyncExternalStore` still subscribes, and drafts -> DB -> resources registration still runs inside `registry.batch()`.
The boundary fallback test still passes after the rework, so `EffectErrorBoundary` continues to log the effect name and render the explicit fallback prop on runtime failure.

No changes were made to param validation in this pass; the existing coercion test stayed green, confirming the utility still lives in `effects/` and handles number, boolean, select, and color coercion.
No changes were made to `wrapWithClipEffects` in this pass, and the full suite rerun did not surface any contract regressions around continuous-vs-transition props or effect names.
No changes were made to `EffectLayerSequence` in this pass; the rerun suite kept its existing contract and fallback behavior intact.
No preview-path edits were needed for the rework. `wrapPreviewEffect` remains unchanged and `PREVIEW_FPS` is still hardcoded at 30 per the accepted tradeoff.
No changes were needed to the legacy hook deprecation in this pass; the file remains unchanged from the earlier implementation.

The existing registry-internals coverage still passes, and the new integration test now adds the missing rendered-tree proof by going through the real `useEffectRegistry` path and waiting for the DOM to update.
The full `src/tools/video-editor/` vitest run passed again with 22 files and 71 tests, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and no caller signature changes were introduced.
The temporary repro script passed, then the targeted and full verification runs passed again, so this rework did not uncover any further regressions that needed follow-up.
